nativeRange.habitat: Sandy reef-rubble zones and soft substrates in the Eastern Pacific, where individuals occupy empty polychaete worm tubes as shelter and territorial anchoring points.

decorPreferences: live rock with crevices and tunnels

diet.notes: Has been documented eating mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, frozen meaty preparations, and other small protein-rich foods. Generally tends to accept frozen and prepared marine meaty items without significant difficulty.

avoidWith: fish that compete for tube territories

summary: The Orangethroat Pikeblenny is a small Eastern Pacific blenny known for claiming empty worm tubes in reef rubble as its home base. Males display a vivid orange throat and perform dramatic territorial displays; the species is generally considered reef-safe and well suited to beginner reef aquariums.
careNotes: The Orangethroat Pikeblenny is commonly found in sandy reef-rubble zones from the Gulf of California to southern California, where it has been reported inhabiting empty polychaete worm tubes as shelter. Providing appropriate tube substitutes — sections of PVC pipe up to 1.3 cm in diameter, or live rock with suitably sized crevices — tends to greatly reduce stress and encourages natural territorial display behavior. While generally regarded as a peaceful community fish, individuals have been known to defend their chosen tube or burrow with notable intensity, and keeping multiple individuals in a small tank may lead to aggression over territories. Males exhibit vivid sexual dichromatism: males display a bright orange throat stripe and bold markings used in dramatic tail-standing and gill-flaring displays, while females tend to show muted, mottled earth tones. A tightly-fitted or weighted aquarium lid is strongly advised, as blennies as a group are widely regarded as prone to jumping. The species' temperature range of 17–28°C reflects its subtropical Eastern Pacific origin, and it can generally tolerate somewhat cooler water than typical tropical reef fish.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (15.0 cm TL matches LiveAquaria's 6 in / 15.24 cm), temperature range (15.8–28°C vs 17.8–27.8°C from LA — averaged to 17–28°C), taxonomy (family Chaenopsidae, order Blenniiformes), and native range (Eastern Pacific, Gulf of California to southern California). FishBase did not provide pH, salinity, or tank-size data.
